original: agent.run() in execution.ts (line 251) passes `sessionRole: "implementer"` for all test strategies it handles (no-test, test-after)
refined: When agent.run() is called in execution.ts for test strategies 'no-test' and 'test-after', the options object passed to agent.run() contains sessionRole === 'implementer'

original: When `ctx.config.review.enabled` is true, execution.ts passes `keepSessionOpen: true` to agent.run()
refined: When ctx.config.review.enabled is true, the options object passed to agent.run() in execution.ts contains keepSessionOpen === true

original: When `ctx.config.execution.rectification?.enabled` is true, execution.ts passes `keepSessionOpen: true` to agent.run()
refined: When ctx.config.execution.rectification.enabled is true, the options object passed to agent.run() in execution.ts contains keepSessionOpen === true

original: When both `ctx.config.review.enabled` and `ctx.config.execution.rectification?.enabled` are falsy, execution.ts passes `keepSessionOpen: false` to agent.run()
refined: When both ctx.config.review.enabled and ctx.config.execution.rectification?.enabled are falsy, the options object passed to agent.run() in execution.ts contains keepSessionOpen === false

original: buildSessionName(storyWorkdir, ctx.prd.feature, ctx.story.id, "implementer") produces the same name as buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, story.id, "implementer") in autofix.ts:301 for identical inputs
refined: Given identical inputs (workdir, featureName, storyId, 'implementer'), buildSessionName() returns the same string value when called from execution.ts and from autofix.ts:301

original: runSemanticReview() signature includes `featureName?: string` as a parameter
refined: The function signature of runSemanticReview() in the semantic review module contains a parameter named `featureName` of type `string | undefined` (optional string). Verified by reading the source file and confirming the parameter exists in the function declaration.

original: runReview() in runner.ts signature includes `featureName?: string` and forwards it to runSemanticReview()
refined: The function signature of runReview() in runner.ts contains `featureName?: string` as a parameter, and the body of runReview() passes that `featureName` value (not a hardcoded string) as an argument to the runSemanticReview() call. Verified by static analysis of runner.ts source.

original: ReviewOrchestrator.review() in orchestrator.ts signature includes `featureName?: string` and forwards it to runReview()
refined: The method signature of ReviewOrchestrator.review() in orchestrator.ts includes `featureName?: string`, and the method body passes that parameter value to the runReview() call. Verified by reading orchestrator.ts source.

original: review.ts stage passes `ctx.prd.feature` as `featureName` to reviewOrchestrator.review()
refined: In the review pipeline stage (review.ts), the call to reviewOrchestrator.review() includes `featureName: ctx.prd.feature` (or equivalent positional argument using ctx.prd.feature). Verified by reading review.ts source and confirming ctx.prd.feature is passed as featureName.

original: The complete() call in semantic.ts uses `buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, story.id, "semantic")` instead of the hardcoded `nax-semantic-${story.id}`
refined: In semantic.ts, the complete() call passes a session name generated by `buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, story.id, "semantic")` rather than the template literal `nax-semantic-${story.id}`. Verified by reading semantic.ts and confirming buildSessionName is invoked with those four arguments at the complete() call site.

original: The debate branch at semantic.ts:392 passes the received `featureName` parameter instead of `story.id` as featureName to createDebateSession()
refined: At the debate branch in semantic.ts (approximately line 392), the createDebateSession() call receives the `featureName` variable (the parameter passed into the enclosing function) as its featureName argument, not the literal `story.id`. Verified by reading semantic.ts and confirming createDebateSession is called with featureName parameter value, not story.id.

original: When featureName is undefined, buildSessionName() is still called and a debug log is emitted: "featureName missing — semantic session name will not include feature"
refined: When featureName is undefined at the point of building the session name in semantic.ts: (1) buildSessionName() is still invoked (not short-circuited), and (2) a debug-level log statement emits the message "featureName missing — semantic session name will not include feature". Verified by reading the source and confirming both the function call and the logger.debug/log call with that exact message string exist in the undefined-featureName code path.

original: The literal string `nax-semantic-${story.id}` no longer appears anywhere in the codebase
refined: A grep/search of the entire codebase for the pattern `nax-semantic-${story.id}` (or its escaped equivalent `nax-semantic-\$\{story\.id\}`) returns zero matches across all source files. Verified by running a repository-wide text search.

original: runSemanticReview() calls agent.run() instead of agent.complete() for the non-debate code path
refined: In the non-debate code path of runSemanticReview(), agent.run() is invoked and agent.complete() is not invoked; verified by spying on both methods and asserting agent.run.called === true and agent.complete.called === false when reviewDebateEnabled is false

original: agent.run() receives `acpSessionName: buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, storyId, "implementer")` targeting the implementer session
refined: The options object passed to agent.run() contains acpSessionName equal to the return value of buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, storyId, 'implementer'); verified by capturing the call arguments and asserting options.acpSessionName === buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, storyId, 'implementer')

original: agent.run() passes `keepSessionOpen: false` since semantic review is the last consumer
refined: The options object passed to agent.run() contains keepSessionOpen === false; verified by capturing call arguments and asserting options.keepSessionOpen === false

original: runSemanticReview() receives `workdir` (already present) and `featureName` (from US-002) and uses both in buildSessionName()
refined: runSemanticReview() accepts a featureName parameter in its options/arguments, and when building the session name, both workdir and featureName are passed as arguments to buildSessionName(); verified by asserting featureName appears in the function signature and buildSessionName is called with (workdir, featureName, storyId, 'implementer')

original: When agent.run() creates a new session (implementer session not found), a debug log is emitted: "implementer session not found — semantic review running in new session"
refined: When the AgentRunResult indicates a new session was created (i.e., the implementer session was not resumed), logger.debug is called with a message matching 'implementer session not found — semantic review running in new session'; verified by asserting logger.debug was called with that exact string in the new-session scenario

original: The response extraction from agent.run() result correctly populates `rawResponse` — handles AgentRunResult.output instead of CompleteResult
refined: After agent.run() resolves, rawResponse is assigned from AgentRunResult.output (not from a CompleteResult property); verified by mocking agent.run() to return { output: '<mock-text>' } and asserting rawResponse === '<mock-text>'

original: ReviewCheckResult shape returned by runSemanticReview() is unchanged — callers receive the same interface
refined: The object returned by runSemanticReview() satisfies the ReviewCheckResult TypeScript interface with all previously required fields present and typed correctly; verified by TypeScript compilation (tsc --noEmit) passing without errors on existing callers and by asserting the returned object contains the same top-level keys as the prior implementation

original: The debate path (reviewDebateEnabled branch at semantic.ts:382) is not modified by this story
refined: The code block executed when reviewDebateEnabled is true at semantic.ts:382 is byte-for-byte identical to the pre-story baseline; verified by a git diff showing no changes within the reviewDebateEnabled branch, and by a test asserting that when reviewDebateEnabled is true, agent.complete() (not agent.run()) is still invoked

original: resolveOutcome() signature adds `workdir?: string` and `featureName?: string` parameters
refined: resolveOutcome() function signature includes optional parameters `workdir?: string` and `featureName?: string`; calling resolveOutcome() without these parameters compiles and behaves identically to the previous signature

original: synthesisResolver() receives `acpSessionName: buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, storyId, "implementer")` in completeOptions when workdir is defined
refined: When resolveOutcome() is called with a defined workdir, the completeOptions passed to synthesisResolver() contains an `acpSessionName` property equal to buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, storyId, "implementer")

original: judgeResolver() receives `acpSessionName: buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, storyId, "implementer")` in completeOptions when workdir is defined
refined: When resolveOutcome() is called with a defined workdir, the completeOptions passed to judgeResolver() contains an `acpSessionName` property equal to buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, storyId, "implementer")

original: The call site in session-stateful.ts passes `ctx.workdir` and `ctx.featureName` to resolveOutcome()
refined: In session-stateful.ts, the call to resolveOutcome() includes ctx.workdir as the workdir argument and ctx.featureName as the featureName argument

original: When resolveOutcome() is called with workdir defined but resolver type is majority-fail-closed or majority-fail-open, a warn log is emitted: "majority resolver does not support implementer session resumption — switch to synthesis or custom resolver for context-aware semantic review"
refined: When resolveOutcome() receives a defined workdir and resolverType is 'majority-fail-closed' or 'majority-fail-open', logger.warn is called with the message "majority resolver does not support implementer session resumption — switch to synthesis or custom resolver for context-aware semantic review"

original: majority resolver behavior is unchanged — it returns vote count with resolverCostUsd: 0
refined: majorityResolver() returns an object containing a votes array with correct vote counts and a resolverCostUsd field equal to 0, regardless of whether workdir is defined or undefined

original: When workdir is undefined, acpSessionName is not passed to synthesisResolver() or judgeResolver() (omitted from completeOptions)
refined: When resolveOutcome() is called with workdir as undefined, the completeOptions objects passed to synthesisResolver() and judgeResolver() do not contain an `acpSessionName` property

original: DebateResult shape is unchanged — callers receive the same interface
refined: The TypeScript type DebateResult has the same properties and types before and after the change; no existing property is added, removed, or type-changed; all existing callers of resolveOutcome() compile without type errors
